subject: Geography
title: Kashmir
description: A quick, practical guide to Kashmir.

hook.text: India and Pakistan have fought three wars over Kashmir, a mountainous region both claim.

buildup.text: In 1947, the Hindu ruler of Muslim-majority Kashmir chose India. Pakistan invaded.

discovery.text: The 'Line of Control' divides Kashmir today—not a border, but a ceasefire line.

twist.text: Both nations are nuclear-armed. Kashmir is the world's most dangerous territorial dispute.

climax.text: Millions of Kashmiris live under heavy military presence on both sides of the line.

punchline.text: A ceasefire line, not a border—and still no peace.

quiz.question: What divides Kashmir between India and Pakistan?
options: The Line of Control, a ceasefire line
An international border agreed by both
